root_text.an3.8:1.3: Sabyābajjhena kāyakammena, sabyābajjhena vacīkammena, sabyābajjhena manokammena …pe… abyābajjhena kāyakammena, abyābajjhena vacīkammena, abyābajjhena manokammena.
root_text.an3.8:1.4: Imehi, kho, bhikkhave, tīhi dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito veditabbo.
root_text.an3.8:2.2: ‘yehi tīhi dhammehi samannāgato bālo veditabbo te tayo dhamme abhinivajjetvā, yehi tīhi dhammehi samannāgato paṇḍito veditabbo te tayo dhamme samādāya vattissāmā’ti.

translation_text.an3.8:1.1: “A fool is known by three things.
translation_text.an3.8:1.3: Hurtful deeds by way of body, speech, and mind. … An astute person is known by kind deeds by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.8:1.4: These are the three things by which an astute person is known.
translation_text.an3.8:2.1: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an3.8:2.2: ‘We will shun the three qualities by which a fool is known, and we will undertake and follow the three qualities by which an astute person is known.’
